content: LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the spaced retrieval practice framework implicit in high school language learning vocabulary reflection (the linguistic sense of reflexion) to improve long-term performance on complex reasoning tasks, rather than only reflecting on recent errors immediately after generation. Just as learners repeatedly reflect on new vocabulary over spaced intervals to encode it into long-term memory, LLMs can repeatedly revisit and reflect on past solved and failed complex problems across intervals to refine their dynamic memory.
